item.aggregated_output:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Summarize text input. Complete the functions in this file."""
import argparse
import json
import re
import sys


def summarize(text, limit, prefix=None):
    raise NotImplementedError("implement summarize")


def main(argv=None):
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Summarize a UTF-8 text file")
    parser.add_argument("--input", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=5)
    parser.add_argument("--prefix")
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    if args.limit < 1:
        parser.error("--limit must be positive")
    try:
        with open(args.input, encoding="utf-8") as source:
            text = source.read()
    except OSError as error:
        parser.error(str(error))
    print(json.dumps(summarize(text, args.limit, args.prefix), separators=(",", ":")))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

item.text: I found one error-path gap in the scaffold as well: invalid UTF-8 currently produces a traceback. The implementation will catch that alongside ordinary read failures so stderr stays concise.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc 'python3 -m py_compile text_report.py
work=$(mktemp -d)
printf '"'Apple banana apple.\\n   \\nApricot BANANA"'!'"\\n42 pear-shaped\\n' > \""'$work/input.txt"
python3 text_report.py --input "$work/input.txt"
python3 text_report.py --input "$work/input.txt" --limit 2 --prefix AP
python3 text_report.py --input "$work/input.txt" --limit 0 >"$work/out" 2>"$work/err"; code=$?; printf '"'invalid-limit-exit=%s\\n' \""'$code"; sed -n '"'1,4p' \""'$work/err"
printf '"'\\377' > \""'$work/bad.txt"
python3 text_report.py --input "$work/bad.txt" >"$work/out" 2>"$work/err"; code=$?; printf '"'invalid-utf8-exit=%s\\n' \""'$code"; sed -n '"'1,4p' \""'$work/err"
python3 -m unittest discover -v'

item.text: Core behavior and failure cases are producing the expected deterministic JSON. There are no repository tests present; I’m doing a final assertion-based check, including sorting ties, ASCII-only tokenization, blank-line handling, prefixes, and nonzero error exits.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=[REDACTED] python3 -m py_compile text_report.py
python3 - <<'PY'
from text_report import summarize

assert summarize(\"B b a A c\\n\\n  \\n123 -- élan\", 5) == {
    \"line_count\": 2,
    \"word_count\": 7,
    \"top_words\": [[\"a\", 2], [\"b\", 2], [\"c\", 1], [\"lan\", 1], [\"n\", 1]],
}
assert summarize(\"Alpha ALPINE beta alphabet\", 2, \"aLp\") == {
    \"line_count\": 1,
    \"word_count\": 3,
    \"top_words\": [[\"alpha\", 1], [\"alphabet\", 1]],
}
assert summarize(\"\\n\\t\\n\", 5) == {
    \"line_count\": 0,
    \"word_count\": 0,
    \"top_words\": [],
}
print(\"assertion checks passed\")
PY
work="'$(mktemp -d)
printf '"'One two ONE\\n\\nThree\\n' > \""'$work/input.txt"
actual=$(python3 text_report.py --input "$work/input.txt")
test "$actual" = '"'{\"line_count\":2,\"word_count\":4,\"top_words\":[[\"one\",2],[\"three\",1],[\"two\",1]]}'
python3 text_report.py --input \""'$work/missing.txt" >"$work/out" 2>"$work/err"; test $? -ne 0
test ! -s "$work/out"
printf '"'CLI checks passed\\n'
sed -n '1,220p' text_report.py"

item.text:
Implemented [text_report.py]([REDACTED]).

It now supports:

- Ignoring blank lines
- ASCII-letter word extraction and lowercase counting
- Deterministic count/word sorting
- `--limit` and case-insensitive `--prefix`
- Concise errors for invalid arguments, missing files, and invalid UTF-8

Validation passed with compilation, assertion-based coverage, and CLI error checks. No bundled unit tests were present.
